sources_inspected: recent repository commits and owner-scoped queue updates

observed: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is a historical baseline while later owner-scoped updates carry subsequent state transitions.
M-005 specified deterministic reconciliation but did not yet provide executable behavior.
A handoff on a completed item proposes subsequent work and does not reopen that item.
inferred: The live queue should be derived from baseline plus additive updates rather than manually overwritten.
proposed: Add a repository workflow that runs the reconciler tests and publishes a generated current-view artifact.
superseded: Treating ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone as the complete current queue.
unresolved: The executable currently accepts preloaded records; repository file discovery and commit-ancestry lookup are not yet wired.
No canonical generated-current-view path has been selected.

contradictions_resolved.apparent_conflict: A baseline item can remain active while a later owner update marks it completed.
contradictions_resolved.resolution: Retain both records, mark the earlier state superseded, and derive completed only when ordering is decisive.

contradictions_resolved.apparent_conflict: Two same-time updates can disagree about status.
contradictions_resolved.resolution: Emit an unresolved conflict and omit an arbitrary current winner.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files
cases: later completion supersedes active baseline while preserving both
current state retains source and commit provenance
